summary: Complete testing of edit functionality for beneficiaries and documents. Fixed critical bug: 'Form' was not imported in server.py causing backend startup failure. All edit features working correctly.
passed_tests: Backend: PUT /api/beneficiaries/{id} - Updates all beneficiary fields correctly ✓
Backend: PUT /api/beneficiaries/{id} - Returns 404 for non-existent beneficiary ✓
Backend: PUT /api/documents/{id} - Updates document name, category, notes ✓
Backend: PUT /api/documents/{id} - Returns 404 for non-existent document ✓
Backend: PUT /api/documents/{id} - Partial update preserves unchanged fields ✓
Frontend: Beneficiaries page shows 3 beneficiary cards ✓
Frontend: Edit button appears on hover over beneficiary card ✓
Frontend: Edit Beneficiary modal opens with pre-filled data ✓
Frontend: Modal shows all fields (name, relation, DOB, address, etc.) ✓
Frontend: Document Vault shows uploaded documents ✓
Frontend: Edit button appears on hover over document card ✓
Frontend: Edit Document modal opens with pre-filled name, category, notes ✓
Frontend: Save Changes button available in document edit modal ✓
seed_data_creation: Uploaded test document 'Test Document' for document edit testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Edit functionality is fully implemented and working. Beneficiary edit: hover over card to see edit button (pencil icon), click to open modal with all demographic fields pre-filled. Document edit: hover over document card to see edit button, click to open modal with name/category/notes fields. Backend uses Form data (not JSON) for document updates.
fix_applied: Added missing 'Form' import from fastapi in server.py line 1. The update_document endpoint at line 2201 uses Form() for parameters but Form was not imported, causing NameError on backend startup.
screenshots: .screenshots/beneficiaries_page.png - Shows 3 beneficiary cards with edit/delete buttons on hover
.screenshots/edit_modal.png - Edit Beneficiary modal with pre-filled data
.screenshots/document_edit_modal.png - Edit Document modal with name/category/notes fields
